name: RAG-Grounded Chat
description: 2-node reference workflow demonstrating the v1 `host.knowledge` spec extension. Retrieves relevant chunks from a knowledge base, builds a grounding-instructed user prompt with `[#N]` citation markers, and routes to an AI chat completion that cites its sources. Output carries the AI answer + the `citations[]` array so the calling UI can render footnotes. Replaces ~150 LOC of ad-hoc RAG plumbing with two registered packs composed declaratively.

config.systemPrompt: You answer using ONLY the sources provided in the user message. When you use information from a source, cite it as [#N] inline (matching the marker in the Sources block). If the sources don't contain the answer, say so honestly — do not invent facts.

variables.name: userQuestion
variables.description: The end-user's question to ground against the knowledge base.

variables.name: retrievalQuery
variables.description: Optional retrieval-query override. When omitted, defaults to userQuestion. Use when the question's natural-language form would retrieve poorly (e.g., 'How does it work?' → 'product onboarding flow').

variables.name: workspaceId
variables.description: Workspace scope passed through to ctx.knowledge.retrieve. Host enforces tenant isolation.

variables.name: collectionIds
variables.description: Optional collection-ID filter. When omitted, retrieval spans every collection visible to the principal.

variables.name: aiProvider
variables.description: AI provider identifier from `capabilities.aiProviders.supported` (e.g., 'anthropic', 'openai', 'gemini'). Host routes the call via its BYOK/configured-key path.
